behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: Real failures are likely at the Git/PR-boundary: commit reachability vs `main`, commit-age extraction, and PR absence must be evaluated from actual repo state and real branch metadata. In-process fakes can miss Git graph/time semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp Git repo with `main` and a feature branch containing older commits not reachable from `main`; ensure no PR exists for that branch in the test PR source; run staleness detection end-to-end and assert it returns stale. Repeat with a recent commit to assert threshold behavior.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: This behavior crosses bootstrap/runtime wiring and output emission at session start, which is typically process/env driven. Unit tests of formatter logic would miss bootstrap hook and real startup behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Start a real session/bootstrap entrypoint in a controlled repo where current branch is stale; assert emitted warning appears on startup output and includes actionable branch context (branch name, stale reason/age, and next step).

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: The behavior is explicitly a local deterministic mapping function from computed state to labels. No module boundary, infra, subprocess, or model output is required.
behaviors.test_description: Table-drive inputs of computed branch-state structs into the classifier and assert exact labels `clean` or `stale-no-pr`, including edge values around threshold flags; run repeatedly to confirm determinism.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text explicitly requires live repo and PR state plus rendered command output. Mocking would hide real failures in command wiring, repo inspection, and external PR lookup integration.
behaviors.test_description: Run `/kaizen-wip` in an environment with real repo state and real/realistic PR backend state: scenario A clean branch, scenario B commits without PR. Assert rendered output labels are exactly `clean` vs `STALE` with expected phrasing.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure is local module handoff: staleness logic must consume PR-state outcomes (open/merged) correctly and suppress stale-no-PR. Real OS/subprocess/network is not required by the behavior text itself.
behaviors.test_description: Wire staleness evaluator with PR-state provider in-process; feed branch states mapped to PR statuses `open` and `merged`; assert classifier never returns `stale-no-pr` for those cases, including aged branches and non-main commits.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Higher levels rejected: behavior text only states PR state categories (`open or merged PRs`), not live API/network/subprocess requirements.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: Rebase/squash correctness depends on true Git history rewriting semantics and changed hashes. Synthetic mocks often miss ancestry/merge-base edge cases that only appear with real Git operations.
behaviors.test_description: In a real Git repo, create stale-looking branch, then perform rebase and squash-merge variants that rewrite commit hashes while integrating work. Run detection after each operation and assert branch is no longer classified as unPRed stale work.
